questions.prompt: Why is account auto-linking by email risky if email isn't verified?

choices.text: Account takeover vector. Attacker creates an account with the victim's email (unverified), then victim signs in with their real Google account using the same email. Auto-linking would merge them — attacker now has access to victim's account, including any history from the attacker's pre-existing account. Production-safe approach: only auto-link when both identities have verified emails, or keep auto-link off entirely and expose 'link more providers' in account settings requiring re-auth before linking.

questions.explanation: Unverified email + auto-link = takeover vector. Require verification or manual linking.

questions.prompt: B2B SaaS chasing enterprise deals at $50k+ ACVs. Auth requirement?

choices.text: SAML SSO. Enterprise customers require their IT to manage user access centrally via Okta, Azure AD, Google Workspace, OneLogin, or JumpCloud. Without SAML, you lose deals at this level — it's a checkbox item in enterprise procurement. Supabase supports SAML on Pro plans and above. Setup per customer: collect their SAML metadata, configure a connection scoped to their domain, expose a `signInWithSSO({ domain })` button. JIT provisioning creates users on first login. SCIM for full lifecycle management is the next step up but rarely blocks deals.

questions.explanation: SAML SSO is non-negotiable for enterprise deals at $50k+ ACVs.
